techniques.evidence_quote: the 3rd party Amazon ECS task definition that the customer was using, was unintentionally exposing a web interface to the internet. That interface allowed unauthenticated users to run commands on the system.
That interface allowed unauthenticated users to run commands on the system.
there were a number of attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role that was associated with the Amazon ECS task.

techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: there were a number of attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role that was associated with the Amazon ECS task.
The majority of actions were attempting to launch multiple Amazon EC2 instances via RunInstances calls.
the threat actor decided to leave with just the one Amazon ECS task running crypto mining rather than a larger number of Amazon EC2 instances.

techniques.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
techniques.evidence_quote: The majority of actions were attempting to launch multiple Amazon EC2 instances via RunInstances calls. Every one of these actions, along with the other actions attempted, failed with either a Client.UnauthorizedOperation or an AccessDenied error message.

attack_chain.description: The attacker discovered and exploited a web interface unintentionally exposed to the internet by a third-party ECS task definition. The interface required no authentication and allowed arbitrary command execution.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the 3rd party Amazon ECS task definition that the customer was using, was unintentionally exposing a web interface to the internet. That interface allowed unauthenticated users to run commands on the system.

attack_chain.description: Using the exposed unauthenticated web interface, the attacker executed commands within the running ECS container, achieving remote code execution on the task.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: That interface allowed unauthenticated users to run commands on the system.

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: The attacker obtained the Task IAM role credentials from within the ECS task environment, most likely via the container metadata/credentials endpoint, in order to make AWS API calls.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: there were a number of attempts to use the credentials of the Task IAM role that was associated with the Amazon ECS task.

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: The attacker attempted to launch multiple EC2 instances via RunInstances API calls using the stolen Task IAM role credentials, presumably to deploy cryptomining workloads at scale. All attempts were denied due to the IAM role only permitting S3 actions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The majority of actions were attempting to launch multiple Amazon EC2 instances via RunInstances calls. Every one of these actions, along with the other actions attempted, failed with either a Client.UnauthorizedOperation or an AccessDenied error message.

attack_chain.description: Failing to launch additional EC2 instances, the attacker fell back to running a cryptomining process within the single compromised ECS task. Amazon GuardDuty detected this activity via CryptoCurrency:EC2/BitcoinTool findings.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the threat actor decided to leave with just the one Amazon ECS task running crypto mining rather than a larger number of Amazon EC2 instances.

initial_access_vector: Unauthenticated RCE via a web interface unintentionally exposed to the internet by a third-party ECS task definition
persistence_mechanism: None identified; the attacker re-exploited the same vulnerability after the customer rebuilt resources, rather than establishing an independent persistence mechanism
privilege_escalation: Attacker accessed the Task IAM role credentials associated with the ECS task (likely via the container credentials endpoint), effectively inheriting the cloud permissions of that role
